ideas.label: m=1 stability of rotating helical Z-pinch is closed by the H3 quartic Alfvén minimum
ideas.description: Atlas (Kumar et al. 2026) extends the Hameiri framework to the rotating helical Z-pinch with non-monotonic Bz and proves that under five necessary and sufficient constraints H1-H5, the m=1 ideal-MHD growth rate is exactly zero in the asymptotic d_i/R→0 limit. The structural ingredient absent from prior shear-flow-only treatments is the H3 quartic minimum of F(r)=(mBθ+krBz)/r, which produces a spectral gap separating the discrete kink mode from the Alfvén continuum. The static obstruction of Newcomb-Kadomtsev does not extend across this regime, and the linear-shear-only result of Angus 2020 is silent on profile families that satisfy H3.

scope.regime: rotating helical cylindrical Z-pinch, m=1 mode, ideal MHD, d_i/R→0
conditions: non-monotonic Bz with quartic F minimum

contributing_claims.label: 5 constraints H1-H5 are necessary and sufficient for m=1 stability in rotating helical Z-pinch
H3 quartic Alfvén minimum is the structural ingredient absent from shear-flow-only treatments
Discrete spectrum near quartic continuum minimum accumulates as n^-4 (exponent sharp)
Bi-orthogonal Picone identity generalizes Newcomb node-counting to non-self-adjoint operators
Below SARI bound M_A<1, ideal-MHD m=1 growth rate is exactly zero in asymptotic d_i/R→0 regime
Framework simultaneously closes five prior conjectures (Bondeson-Iacono helical, Hameiri Sturmian, n^-4 sharpness, SARI exclusion, kink obstruction)
RIGID-BELTRAMI-A double-Beltrami equilibrium achieves <β>=12.3% with all H1-H5 satisfied
Static Z-pinch at finite pressure cannot be linearly stable to m=1 in ideal MHD
contributing_claims.rewrite_description: N_01 narrowed: m=1 obstruction holds in STATIC Z-pinch at finite pressure (out-of-regime weak for rotating helical).
contributing_claims.weakness_invoked: static assumption — extension to rotating helical is non-trivial

contributing_claims.label: Linear shear flow alone does NOT stabilize m=1 kink across realistic Z-pinch profile classes
contributing_claims.rewrite_description: AN_01 narrowed: linear axial shear alone (without H3 quartic structure) does not stabilize m=1 across tested profile families.
contributing_claims.weakness_invoked: tested profile families lack H3 quartic minimum — Atlas's structural ingredient

contributing_claims.label: In toroidal/cylindrical geometry, rotation mitigates but does not eliminate kink at finite pressure
contributing_claims.rewrite_description: B_01 narrowed: toroidal rotation alone (without H3 quartic + helical shear) mitigates but doesn't eliminate kink.
contributing_claims.weakness_invoked: no non-monotonic Bz or helical sheared flow tested

contributing_evidence.label: |γ_m=1| < 8e-12 τ_A^-1 on RIGID-BELTRAMI-A at <β>=12.3%
|F''(r*)|/F_max = 0.41/R^2 on RIGID-BELTRAMI-A confirms H3 quartic
All 120 (m,k) points satisfy |γ|<1e-11 τ_A^-1; worst case (2,0.69): 5.8e-12
Violating H1 (q_min=0.92) produces unstable kink γ ≈ 0.18 τ_A^-1
Violating H3 (monotonic F) gives only n^-2 Suydam accumulation; γ ≈ 4.2e-3 τ_A^-1
ε-pseudospectrum boundary < 4.1e-7 τ_A^-1 in upper half-plane (no transient growth)
Static finite-β Z-pinch has unstable m=1 spectrum (Suydam-style)
m=1 kink remains unstable γ ~ O(v_i/a) at all tested shear levels on realistic profiles
Toroidal rotation mitigates but doesn't eliminate kink (cylindrical analog)

tensions_resolved.after: Newcomb statement narrowed to STATIC regime; Atlas adds rotation+helical Bz with H3 as new structural ingredient

tensions_resolved.before: Angus 2020: linear shear flow doesn't stabilize m=1 across realistic profile families
tensions_resolved.after: Angus statement narrowed to NO-H3 profile families; Atlas's H3 is the missing ingredient

tensions_resolved.before: Bondeson-Iacono 1989: toroidal rotation alone doesn't eliminate kink
tensions_resolved.after: Bondeson narrowed to TOROIDAL-without-H3 case; helical sheared flow + non-monotonic Bz is the missing ingredient

Run finite-d_i/R Hall-MHD eigensolver on RIGID-BELTRAMI-A; track |γ_m=1| as function of d_i/R
Compare with Sainterme-Ebrahimi 2026 global whistler instability regime and assess overlap

Develop polarimetry diagnostic with δr/R<0.05 resolution to verify F(r) quartic structure near r*

transitions_out.relation: structurally extends — Atlas's framework includes the static case as a special-case limit
asymptotic boundary — Atlas's d_i/R→0 result is the reference for Hall corrections
operating boundary — Atlas's stability ends at M_A=1 where SARI takes over

ideas.label: The static finite-β m=1 obstruction is genuine and well-characterized — it survives as a special-case limit
ideas.description: The classical Newcomb-Kadomtsev result establishes that the static finite-pressure Z-pinch is linearly unstable to m=1. This is reproduced and benchmarked by Atlas (the ARPACK pipeline recovers Newcomb 1960 eigenvalues to 10^-5 relative accuracy). The result is not weakened by Atlas — it is contextualized as the limit-case where H3 is absent (monotonic F profile). Bondeson-Iacono's toroidal flow analysis sits in an adjacent regime where rotation mitigates but doesn't eliminate the kink, again because the H3 ingredient is missing. The static obstruction is genuine and remains the correct prediction in its native regime.

scope.regime: static or non-helical-rotating Z-pinch at finite pressure
scope.framework: Frieman-Rotenberg self-adjoint MHD operator (no flow) → Newcomb counting theorem
conditions: no rotation OR rotation without H3

complementary — m=0 stabilization closes a different mode, Newcomb-Kadomtsev silent on m=0 in this regime

ideas.label: Hall corrections at finite d_i/R regularize the H3 singularity over a layer of thickness d_i
ideas.description: The H3 quartic minimum of F(r) is a singular feature of ideal MHD. At finite ion skin depth d_i, Hall corrections regularize this singularity over a layer of thickness ~d_i (Shiraishi 2005). For compact pinches with d_i/R~0.1, this is a regime change rather than a perturbation. Recent work (Mahajan-Lingam 2024, Sainterme-Ebrahimi 2026) characterizes the Hall-MHD wave spectrum as fundamentally distinct from MHD at d_i scales, with global whistler instabilities growing faster than ideal-MHD modes. Atlas's framework provides the unperturbed reference against which Hall corrections are to be computed in companion work.

scope.regime: finite d_i/R, Hall-MHD regime around H3 layer
scope.framework: Hall MHD, symmetrizable but non-Hermitian operator

contributing_claims.label: Hall effect regularizes the Alfvén singularity over a layer of thickness ~d_i
Hall MHD waves are fundamentally distinct from MHD waves at d_i scales
In Hall regime, global whistler instabilities grow significantly faster than ideal-MHD modes
Below SARI bound M_A<1, ideal-MHD m=1 growth rate is exactly zero in asymptotic d_i/R→0 regime
H3 quartic Alfvén minimum is the structural ingredient absent from shear-flow-only treatments

contributing_evidence.label: Hall-MHD solutions converge to ideal MHD outside d_i-scale neighborhood of singularity
Global whistler instabilities grow significantly faster than ideal-MHD modes at d_i/L ~ few%
|γ_m=1| < 8e-12 τ_A^-1 on RIGID-BELTRAMI-A at <β>=12.3%

ideas.label: Atlas's regime ends at M_A=1 where SARI continuum overlap takes over
ideas.description: The Super-Alfvénic Rotational Instability (Goedbloed-Keppens 2022, Brughmans-Keppens-Goedbloed 2024) emerges from overlap of forward and backward Doppler-shifted Alfvén continua when M_A ≥ 1. Atlas's stability proof requires the SARI exclusion bound M_A < 1 — pointwise, on the full radial domain. Atlas proves the M_A<1 exclusion side of the Goedbloed-Keppens conjecture, while the M_A≥1 side is the unstable regime that links pinch physics to accretion-disk theory. The experimental observation of axisymmetric SMRI (Wang et al. 2022) is in the axisymmetric (m=0) and SMRI (not SARI) regime — separate but related.

scope.regime: rotating plasma at varying M_A; transition at M_A=1

contributing_claims.label: Super-Alfvénic Rotational Instability (SARI) emerges from overlap of forward/backward Doppler-shifted Alfvén continua
At M_A ≥ 1 continuum overlap opens uncontrolled SARI spectrum

Axisymmetric SMRI directly observed in liquid-metal Taylor-Couette experiment

Essential spectrum of ideal MHD with flow consists of forward/backward Doppler-shifted Alfvén + slow continua

|γ_m=1| < 8e-12 τ_A^-1 on RIGID-BELTRAMI-A at <β>=12.3%
Ideal MHD with flow has forward/backward Doppler-shifted Alfvén continua governing essential spectrum

transitions_out.relation: operating boundary — Atlas's stability is for M_A<1
alternative defeat mechanism — Hall vs SARI as ways out of Atlas-stable region

ideas.label: Shumlak's m=0 sheared-flow stabilization is complementary, not competing — together both modes are closed
ideas.description: Shumlak & Hartman 1995 established that sheared axial flow above v'/(kV_A) ≈ 0.1 stabilizes the m=0 sausage in static Z-pinches; the ZaP experiment (Shumlak 2001) and FuZE (Zhang 2019) operationally demonstrate this in long-quiescent plasmas. Atlas explicitly notes that this result is silent on m=1, and that its own H3 ingredient closes the distinct m=1 obstruction. On RIGID-BELTRAMI-A both ingredients are simultaneously present, so the joint configuration potentially closes both modes. The mode families differ (m=0 for Shumlak, m=1 for Atlas), so this is a complementary rather than a generalizing result.

scope.regime: Z-pinch with sheared axial flow (Shumlak) + helical sheared flow with H3 (Atlas)
scope.framework: ideal MHD, two complementary mode-family analyses

contributing_claims.label: Sheared axial flow v'/(kV_A) > ~0.1 stabilizes m=0 sausage mode in static Z-pinch
ZaP experiment observes long stable period (>700× growth time) coincident with sheared sub-Alfvénic flow
Required shear for m=0 stabilization scales as ~c_0(ka)/(ka)·α with α≪1; m=1 needs different mechanism
Kadomtsev's interchange criterion has an entropy-gradient interpretation analogous to Schwarzschild-Ledoux
FuZE experimentally demonstrates sustained ~5μs neutron production during ~16μs quiescent period
Framework simultaneously closes five prior conjectures (Bondeson-Iacono helical, Hameiri Sturmian, n^-4 sharpness, SARI exclusion, kink obstruction)

Kadomtsev marginal-stable profile has entropy-gradient interpretation analogous to Schwarzschild-Ledoux
